docs.title: DonaDataset
docs.text:
DonaDataset is the annotated camera-trap image dataset used to train DonaNet, a YOLO-based neural network for detecting and classifying the mammals that inhabit Doñana National Park (Spain).

Images and labels are hosted on HuggingFace Hub. This repository contains the metadata, class definitions, and utility scripts.

docs.title: Quick download
docs.text:
python scripts/download.py

Or with the HuggingFace CLI:

huggingface-cli download wildintelproject/donadataset \
  --repo-type dataset --local-dir ./data

docs.title: DonaDataset & WildINTEL
docs.text:
DonaDataset is the training dataset behind DonaNet, a YOLO-based neural network developed as part of the WildINTEL project, an initiative dedicated to the automated monitoring of wildlife through artificial intelligence and camera-trap imagery.

The dataset focuses on the mammal species that inhabit Doñana National Park (Spain), one of the most important wetland ecosystems in Europe.

docs.title: Funding
docs.text:
This work is part of the WildINTEL project, funded by the Biodiversa+ Joint Research Call 2022–2023 "Improved transnational monitoring of biodiversity and ecosystem change for science and society (BiodivMon)".

Biodiversa+ is the European co-funded biodiversity partnership supporting excellent research on biodiversity with an impact for policy and society. Biodiversa+ is part of the European Biodiversity Strategy for 2030 that aims to put Europe's biodiversity on a path to recovery by 2030 and is co-funded by the European Commission.

docs.title: Classes
docs.text:
The dataset covers the following mammal species present in Doñana National Park. Class ids match the order in metadata/classes.yaml and metadata/dataset.yaml.

ID Common name Scientific name 0 Red deer Cervus elaphus 1 Fallow deer Dama dama 2 Wild boar Sus scrofa 3 Iberian lynx Lynx pardinus 4 Red fox Vulpes vulpes 5 Egyptian mongoose Herpestes ichneumon 6 European rabbit Oryctolagus cuniculus 7 Badger Meles meles

Adding new classes

To extend the dataset with new species, add entries to metadata/classes.yaml keeping ids contiguous, update metadata/dataset.yaml accordingly, and open a pull request. The CI workflow will verify consistency automatically.

docs.title: Camera traps
docs.text: Images were collected using camera traps deployed across Doñana National Park. Cameras were positioned at known wildlife corridors, water points and feeding areas to maximise species coverage.

docs.title: Image processing
docs.text: Raw images are exported in JPEG format. No colour correction or resizing is applied before annotation — the model receives images at their original resolution.

docs.title: Annotation workflow
docs.text:
Annotations were produced using the YOLO bounding-box format. Each detected animal is labelled with:

  • Class id — species identifier (see Classes)
  • Bounding box — normalised centre coordinates and dimensions

Ambiguous detections (partial occlusion, low confidence) are excluded from the dataset.

docs.title: Dataset splits
docs.text:
Images are split into train / val / test partitions using the DonaNet prepare-dataset command with the following default ratios:

Split Ratio train 70 % val 20 % test 10 %

Stratified sampling is applied to ensure each species is represented in all splits.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: DonaDataset is a collection of camera-trap images annotated in YOLO format for object detection. It covers the mammal species present in Doñana National Park (Huelva, Spain), one of the most important wetland ecosystems in Europe.

docs.title: Storage
docs.text: Images and labels are hosted on HuggingFace Hub at wildintelproject/donadataset.

docs.title: Dataset splits
docs.text: Split Purpose train Model training val Hyperparameter tuning / monitoring test Final evaluation (held-out)

docs.title: Directory layout (after download)
docs.text:
data/
├── train/
│   ├── images/   ← camera-trap images (.jpg)
│   └── labels/   ← YOLO annotations (.txt)
├── val/
│   ├── images/
│   └── labels/
└── test/
    ├── images/
    └── labels/

docs.title: Annotation format
docs.text:
Each label file contains one row per detected animal:

<class_id> <x_center> <y_center> <width> <height>

All coordinates are normalised to [0, 1] relative to the image dimensions.

docs.title: Metadata files
docs.text: File Description metadata/classes.yaml Maps class ids to common and scientific species names metadata/dataset.yaml Ultralytics YOLO config — use directly with donanet train
